notes.note: The letter-heading: "Cicero to Acilius the proconsul, greetings." M'. Acilius Glabrio served as proconsul of Sicily 46–45 BC; the five letters 13.33–13.37 are all addressed to him, recommending Sicilian businessmen and their agents.

notes.anchor: than any man of his rank
notes.note: "Than any man of his rank" — Naso's ordo (social rank) is the equestrian order; the phrase signals that Naso is a Roman knight, not a senator, and calibrates the warmth of the recommendation within the social hierarchy Glabrio would recognise.

notes.anchor: looked after by his freedmen Hilarus, Antigonus, and Demostratus
notes.note: Roman businessmen routinely managed provincial operations through freed slaves acting as procuratores (agents). The Greek names — Hilarus, Antigonus, Demostratus — are typical of the freedman class and reflect the Greek-speaking eastern slave market from which household staff were drawn.
